lastMessage.text: Good day Mattie, please confirm that your door lock is fixed now

recent_messages.text: Good day Mattie, please confirm that your door lock is fixed now
Good morning everyone. Who would be available in the home tomorrow?
Good morning Mattie, would you be available tomorrow?
Great, thank you.
Thank you please let me know about the batteries when you pick up for reimbursement. We would take care of the pest issues.

Cindy,

I don’t mind picking up the batteries to change in the smoke detector.  However pest control needs to come out and spray the house professionally I cannot take care of that. It is an owners responsibility to keep the house in good condition and take care of any maintenance issues. That house makes too much money in rent to not keep this house in good condition. The lock on n my door is a security issue and in my opinion there should be a doorbell camera for everyone in the houses safety. Regarding the basic upkeep to the house such as cleaning the dust off the baseboards cleaning up the mounted dust under the sink, sinks the caulking that’s falling off on the tiles and the shower and multiple of other things. It seems like there has not been any maintenance to this house. So many people come and go and I wonder how long it’s been since the owner or a representative have actually been in there to look at the house, if anyone person wore to view this house to potentially move in I don’t think they would. I know I wouldn’t have. I have been in property management and this is with all due respect, not acceptable. I want to say this again the housemate do cleanup up for themselves such as take out their own trash they scrub the bottom of the shower before they use it. They keep the toilets clean. They washed their dishes other than that nothing else is being cleaned, floors, doors, etc. that’s why it is imperative when you have seven people living in a small house that you have a pro professional come in and clean. I appreciate you reading my message and taking my concerns serious. Just one more thing I want to mention is that the rooms that were converted from the garage do not cool like the rest of the house when I go to the bathroom and then I walk back to my room I can feel the difference and the air temperature maybe that’s why I feel hot and I’m a very very cold blooded person. My office used to make fun of me because I would be at work with a blanket on and a sweater. I look forward to hearing back from you in a couple days once you have a chance to reach out to your or the owner so that you guys can make plans to get pest control out there and a maintenance personnel to secure my door, put a handle on it and find somebody that can clean the house once a week or once every two  weeks. I asked that when you sent out the message regarding the house that it does not look like I came to you and made a complaint because that’s gonna make hostility in the household..

Thank you again for listening and I hope you have a great day and a great rest of the week.
Kind regards
Mattie Garcia
I'll follow up with you directly once each item has been scheduled. Please don't hesitate to reach out if anything else comes up in the meantime.
For Popcorn ceiling I'll flag this to the owner as a repair item.
For Fridge space,  I'll bring this up with the owner as well and have another fridge sent as soon as possible

For Noise/insulation,  Unfortunately, the wall insulation between converted garage rooms isn't something we can fix immediately, but I will send a community courtesy reminder to all members about noise levels, especially late-night hours.

Overnight guests  Per PadSplit policy, guests are not permitted to stay overnight or cohabitate in a member's room. I will be reminding all members of this rule.

I bagged, tied, and stacked bags with everything I could lift. Ex-tenants and neighbors have left many loose baskets, trash, and discarded items in the alley. I can't mess with that. Thank you for the reminder.

Hi everyone,

The city will be coming today to pick up the trash after the recent delay. Please make sure all trash is taken out very early and placed in the designated bins/area.

Important reminders:
• All trash must be **properly bagged and tied** (no loose items)
• Break down boxes before placing them out
• Do not leave trash on the ground — use the bins only
• If bins are full, place bags neatly beside them

If these are not done they won’t pick up the trash. Let’s keep the property clean and make sure everything gets picked up. Thank you!

recent_messages.text: Hi everyone, just a quick update:Princess in room 4 will no longer be living with us and will be moving out. Thank you.

Hi everyone,  

Thank you so much to everyone for who have been keeping the house clean, it is appreciated. This is just a general reminder for everyone to please clean up after yourselves, especially in shared spaces like the kitchen and bathrooms.  

Please wash your dishes after use, wipe down surfaces, throw away trash properly, and avoid leaving personal items in common areas. Keeping the shared spaces clean makes the house more comfortable for everyone.  

Let’s all do our part to maintain a clean and respectful living environment. Thank you.

Hi everyone, quick house reminder to help keep the home comfortable for all padmates.

When cooking, especially foods that create heavy smoke or grease (like fried foods), please make sure the exhaust fan is turned on and the kitchen door remains closed while cooking. You can open the window as well to help prevent smoke and strong food odors from spreading into the rooms.

Also, for safety reasons, windows should not be left open late at night or overnight. If a window is opened briefly to air out the kitchen, please make sure it is closed immediately before going to bed.

Thank you everyone for being considerate of shared spaces.

Hi everyone, just a quick reminder about shared spaces. For safety reasons, please make sure the kitchen windows are closed at night and be mindful of leaving lights on in shared areas when they are not in use. Let’s all work together to keep the home safe and comfortable for everyone. Thank you.

property.description:
Newly-renovated, spacious home on a quiet cul-de-sac. Perfect for professionals looking for a high-quality, stress-free stay.

Why you’ll love it:

All-Inclusive: One weekly payment covers all utilities, high-speed Wi-Fi, and laundry.

Chef’s Kitchen: Fully equipped with modern appliances and a garbage disposal.

Prime Location: Minutes from I-35E, The Crossing Shopping Center, and UNT Dallas.

Parking: Ample driveway spots available.

Professionally managed with house rules to ensure a quiet, respectful environment for everyone. Just bring your clothes and move in!nd you won’t be far from all DeSoto has to offer.

lastMessage.text:
We received your video and we are glad to welcome you to our home. My name is Cyndy and I’m your property manager. Kindly review and adhere to these guidelines for a harmonious living space. Thank you. 1. It is a collective responsibility to keep all shared spaces clean at all times. 2. Clean the kitchen immediately after use. 3. Clean the bathroom/toilet after every use. 4. Take out the trash when full. 5. Observe quiet hours: 10 PM – 7 AM. 6. No guests permitted. 7. Pay rent on time. 8. Respect all housemates’ privacy and space. 9. Lock all doors and windows when leaving. 10. No smoking indoors. 11. No pets allowed. 12. Always attach move-out photos when vacating the house.

https://docs.google.com/document/d/1yItPFcMB7VYSdyupb8zUMMM_7Go8Jyj-/edit?usp=drivesdk&ouid=117120715329483998875&rtpof=true&sd=true

Hi everyone, with the extreme cold and wind chill expected, please monitor the weather closely and take precautions to protect the home. Please drip sinks, keep cabinets under sinks open, and wrap exposed plumbing where possible to prevent frozen pipes. Power outages are possible, so have non-perishable food, water, flashlights, phone chargers, and keep your gas tank at least half full. For safety, please avoid walking or parking under the large pecan tree behind the house due to falling branches—use the grassy side of the driveway when possible. Thank you for helping keep all houses safe.

property.description:
Move into a freshly renovated House today! Close to freeways, food, grocery and a bus station this spacious and newly renovated home is the perfect place to call home. 

The kitchen comes equipped with a fridge, sink, oven, stovetop, as well as dishwasher, making it the perfect spot to whip up a meal or two.

The neighbourhood is near highways, food and public transit while still being quiet and safe and you won’t be far from all Dallas has to offer.

lastMessage.text:
Hi everyone,

For everyone’s safety and comfort, please remember the following house rules:

• No smoking inside the property.
• No bringing in guests.
• Empty trash properly — no loose trash in or around the bins.
• Clean the kitchen immediately after use (wash dishes, wipe counters, and leave the space as you found it).

This is a shared living space, and we all play a part in keeping it safe, clean, and respectful.

Thank you for your cooperation.

lastMessage.text: Hi everyone, with the extreme cold and wind chill expected, please monitor the weather closely and take precautions to protect the home. Please drip sinks, keep cabinets under sinks open, and wrap exposed plumbing where possible to prevent frozen pipes. Power outages are possible, so have non-perishable food, water, flashlights, phone chargers, and keep your gas tank at least half full. For safety, please avoid walking or parking under the large pecan tree behind the house due to falling branches—use the grassy side of the driveway when possible. Thank you for helping keep all houses safe.
